CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU has a workload of any type queued for processing.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has any pending workload present in one of its processing queues. It shows the overall GPU processing load requested by the application.
This counter increments when any workload is present in any processing queue, even if the GPU is stalled waiting for external memory. These cycles are counted as active time even though no progress is being made.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU has a workload of any type queued for processing.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has any pending workload present in one of its processing queues. It shows the overall GPU processing load requested by the application.
This counter increments when any workload is present in any processing queue, even if the GPU is stalled waiting for external memory. These cycles are counted as active time even though no progress is being made.
On Mali-G715 the GPU_ACTIVE hardware counter has an errata which means it erroneously includes cycles for idle CSF asynchronous operations. We recommend use of the {{C::MaliGPUActiveCy}} counter instead of this one.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU has a workload of any type queued for processing.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has any pending workload present in one of its processing queues. It shows the overall GPU processing load requested by the application.
This counter increments when any workload is present in any processing queue, even if the GPU is stalled waiting for external memory. These cycles are counted as active time even though no progress is being made.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when any GPU queue is active.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when any GPU command queue is active with work for the tiler or shader cores.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Command stream doorbell interrupt pending cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that command stream doorbell has an IRQ pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream doorbell IRQ is pending. This interrupt is handled by the GPU MCU firmware.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of interrupts raised by the GPU to the CPU.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments for every interrupt raised by the GPU.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU has a pending interrupt.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has an interrupt pending and is waiting for the CPU to process it.
Cycles with a pending interrupt do not necessarily indicate lost performance because the GPU can process other queued work in parallel. However, if {{C::MaliGPUIRQActiveCy}} are a high percentage of {{C::MaliGPUActiveCy}}, an underlying problem might be preventing the CPU from efficiently handling interrupts. This problem is normally a system integration issue, which an application developer cannot work around.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The IRQ pending utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression defines the IRQ pending utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. In a well-functioning system, this expression should be less than 3% of the total cycles. If the value is much higher than this, a system issue might be preventing the CPU from efficiently handling interrupts.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Non-fragment queue cache flush wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued non-fragment work is waiting for a cache flush.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has non-fragment work queued that can not run or retire because of a pending L2 cache flush.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Non-fragment queue job descriptor read wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number number of cycles when queued non-fragment work is waiting for a descriptor load.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has non-fragment work queued that can not run because of a pending descriptor load from memory.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Non-fragment queue job issue wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued non-fragment work is waiting for an available processor.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has non-fragment work queued that can not run because all processor resources are busy.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Non-fragment queue job dependency wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued non-fragment work is waiting for dependent work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has non-fragment work queued that can not run until dependent work has completed.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Non-fragment queue job finish wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU is waiting for issued non-fragment work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has run out of new non-fragment work to issue, and is waiting for remaining work to complete.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when work is queued in the GPU non-fragment queue.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has any workload present in the non-fragment queue. This queue is used for vertex shaders, tessellation shaders, geometry shaders, fixed-function tiling, and compute shaders. This counter can not disambiguate between these workloads.
In content achieving good parallelism, which is important for overall efficiency of rendering, the highest queue active cycle counter must be similar to the GPU active counter.
This counter increments when any workload is present in the non-fragment processing queue, even if the GPU is stalled waiting for external memory. These cycles are counted as active time even though no progress is being made.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The non-fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression defines the non-fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is close to 100% utilized, then there might be a serialization or dependency problem preventing better overlap across the queues.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the compute queue has work queued.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream compute queue has work queued. The count includes cycles when the queue is stalled because of endpoint contention.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the compute queue is waiting for endpoints to be assigned.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when compute work is queued but cannot start because no endpoints have been assigned.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Compute queue endpoint drain stall cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the compute queue is waiting for endpoints to drain.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when compute work is queued but cannot start because IDVS work is still active on the shared endpoints.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the compute queue is processing work.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression increments every clock cycle when the command stream compute queue has at least one task issued for processing.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The compute queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This expression defines the compute queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized to get the best performance. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is fully utilized, then a serialization or dependency problem might be preventing queue overlap.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the compute queue IRQ was pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream compute queue has an IRQ pending.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the vertex shading queue has work queued.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream vertex shading queue has work queued. The count includes cycles when the queue is stalled because of endpoint contention.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the vertex queue is waiting for endpoints to be assigned.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when vertex work is queued but cannot start because no endpoints have been assigned.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Vertex queue endpoint drain stall cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the vertex queue is waiting for endpoints to drain.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when vertex work is queued but cannot start because compute work is still active on the shared endpoints.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Binning phase queue endpoint drain stall cycles
CounterInfo.GroupHumanName: Binning phase queue endpoint drain stalls
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the binning phase queue is waiting for endpoints to drain.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when binning phase work is queued but cannot start because compute work is still active on the shared endpoints.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the vertex queue is processing work.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression increments every clock cycle when the command stream vertex queue has at least one task issued for processing.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The vertex queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This expression defines the vertex queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized to get the best performance. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is fully utilized, then a serialization or dependency problem might be preventing queue overlap.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the vertex queue IRQ was pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream vertex queue has an IRQ pending.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the binning phase queue has work queued. The binning phase includes position shading, culling, and binning.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream binning phase queue has work queued. The binning phase includes vertex position shading, culling, and primitive binning, and includes cycles when the queue is stalled because of endpoint contention.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Binning phase queue endpoint stall cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the binning phase queue is waiting for endpoints to be assigned.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when binning phase work is queued but cannot start because no endpoints have been assigned. The binning phase includes vertex position shading and primitive binning.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the binning phase queue is processing work. The binning phase includes position shading, culling, and binning.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression increments every clock cycle when the command stream binning phase queue has at least one task issued for processing. The binning phase includes vertex position shading and primitive binning.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The binning phase queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. The binning phase includes position shading, culling, and binning.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This expression defines the binning phase queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. The binning phase includes vertex position shading, culling, and primitive binning.
For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized to get the best performance. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is fully utilized, then a serialization or dependency problem might be preventing queue overlap.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Binning phase queue interrupt pending cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the binning phase queue IRQ was pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream binning phase queue has an IRQ pending.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Fragment queue cache flush wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued fragment work is waiting for a cache flush.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has fragment work queued that can not run or retire because of a pending L2 cache flush.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Fragment queue job descriptor read wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued fragment work is waiting for a descriptor load.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has fragment work queued that can not run because of a pending descriptor load from memory.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Fragment queue job issue wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued fragment work is waiting for an available processor.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has fragment work queued that can not run because all processor resources are busy.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Fragment queue job dependency wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued fragment work is waiting for dependent work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has fragment work queued that can not run until dependent work has completed.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Fragment queue job finish wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU is waiting for issued fragment work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has run out of new fragment work to issue, and is waiting for remaining work to complete.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the fragment queue has work queued.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream fragment queue has work queued. The count includes cycles when the queue is stalled because of endpoint contention.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the fragment queue is waiting for endpoints to be assigned.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when fragment work is queued but cannot start because no endpoints have been assigned.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when work is queued for processing in the GPU fragment queue.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This counter increments every clock cycle when the GPU has any workload present in the fragment queue.
In content achieving good parallelism, which is important for overall efficiency of rendering, the highest queue active cycle counter must be similar to the GPU active counter.
This counter increments when any workload is present in the fragment queue, even if the GPU is stalled waiting for external memory. These cycles are counted as active time even though no progress is being made.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the fragment queue is processing work.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression increments every clock cycle when the command stream fragment queue has at least one task issued for processing.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression defines the fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. For GPU bound content, the GPU queues are expected to process work in parallel. Aim to keep the dominant queue close to 100% utilized. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is close to 100% utilized, then there might be a serialization or dependency problem preventing better queue overlap.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression defines the fragment queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized to get the best performance. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is fully utilized, then a serialization or dependency problem might be preventing scheduling overlap.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the fragment queue IRQ was pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream fragment queue has an IRQ pending.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the main phase queue has work queued. The main phase includes deferred vertex shading and all fragment shading.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream main phase queue has work queued. The main phase includes any deferred vertex processing and all fragment shading, and can include cycles when the queue is stalled because of endpoint contention.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Main phase queue endpoint stall cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles the main phase queue is waiting for endpoints to be assigned.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when main phase work is queued but cannot start because no endpoints have been assigned. The main phase includes any deferred vertex processing and all fragment shading.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the main phase queue is processing work. The main phase includes deferred vertex shading and all fragment shading.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This expression increments every clock cycle when the command stream main phase queue has at least one task issued for processing. The main phase includes any deferred vertex processing and all fragment shading.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The main phase queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. The main phase includes deferred vertex shading and all fragment shading.
CounterInfo.LongDescription:
This expression defines the main phase queue utilization compared against the GPU active cycles. The main phase includes any deferred vertex processing and all fragment shading.
For GPU bound content, it is expected that the GPU queues process work in parallel. The dominant queue must be close to 100% utilized to get the best performance. If no queue is dominant, but the GPU is fully utilized, then a serialization or dependency problem might be preventing queue overlap.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Main phase queue interrupt pending cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles that the main phase queue IRQ was pending.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments every clock cycle when the command stream main phase queue has an IRQ pending.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Reserved queue cache flush wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued reserved work is waiting for a cache flush.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has reserved work queued that can not run or retire because of a pending L2 cache flush.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Reserved queue job descriptor read wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued reserved work is waiting for a descriptor load.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has reserved work queued that can not run because of a pending descriptor load from memory.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Reserved queue job issue wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued reserved work is waiting for an available processor.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has reserved work queued that can not run because all processor resources are busy.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Reserved queue job dependency wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when queued reserved work is waiting for dependent work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has reserved work queued that can not run until dependent work is completed.

CounterInfo.HumanName: Reserved queue job finish wait cycles
CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when the GPU is waiting for issued reserved work to complete.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has run out of new reserved work to issue, and is waiting for remaining work to complete.

CounterInfo.ShortDescription: The number of cycles when work is queued in the GPU reserved queue.
CounterInfo.LongDescription: This counter increments any clock cycle that the GPU has any workload present in the reserved processing queue.
